name: Christmas Lizzies
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Candied cherries, cut in half
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250 degrees.
2. In a large bowl, cream together the brown sugar, butter, soda, cloves, nutmeg, and cinnamon until well combined.
3. Add the beaten eggs and mix well.
4. Stir in the buttermilk, whiskey or brandy, and half of the flour.
5. In a separate bowl, combine the raisins, dates (if using), candied cherries, citron (or lemon and orange peel), and pecans with the remaining flour.
6. Mix into a batter and stir until well combined.
7. Drop by tablespoonfuls (about the size of a small egg) onto greased cookie sheets.
8. Bake for 35 minutes, or until lightly golden brown. Bake one sheet at a time; the remaining batter can stand in the mixing bowl or on the baking sheets without losing its quality.
9. Allow to cool completely before packing into a shallow tin or other container and covering with cheesecloth.
10. Drizzle with whiskey and brandy through the cheesecloth every few days. No need to refrigerate. Can be made up to three weeks ahead of Christmas and eaten immediately after cooling.
